Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class SharingRules

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.SharingRules
public class SharingRules extends Metadata

Java class for SharingRules complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="SharingRules">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="sharingCriteriaRules" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}SharingCriteriaRule" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sharingGuestRules" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}SharingGuestRule" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sharingOwnerRules" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}SharingOwnerRule" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sharingTerritoryRules" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}SharingTerritoryRule" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getSharingCriteriaRules

      public List<SharingCriteriaRule> getSharingCriteriaRules()
      Gets the value of the sharingCriteriaRules property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the sharingCriteriaRules property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSharingCriteriaRules().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list SharingCriteriaRule

      Returns:
      The value of the sharingCriteriaRules property.

    • getSharingGuestRules

      public List<SharingGuestRule> getSharingGuestRules()
      Gets the value of the sharingGuestRules property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the sharingGuestRules property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSharingGuestRules().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list SharingGuestRule

      Returns:
      The value of the sharingGuestRules property.

    • getSharingOwnerRules

      public List<SharingOwnerRule> getSharingOwnerRules()
      Gets the value of the sharingOwnerRules property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the sharingOwnerRules property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSharingOwnerRules().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list SharingOwnerRule

      Returns:
      The value of the sharingOwnerRules property.

    • getSharingTerritoryRules

      public List<SharingTerritoryRule> getSharingTerritoryRules()
      Gets the value of the sharingTerritoryRules property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the sharingTerritoryRules property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getSharingTerritoryRules().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list SharingTerritoryRule

      Returns:
      The value of the sharingTerritoryRules property.
